prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.  Geometry: user-supplied acrolein.xyz (no further relaxation; single-point N, N+1, and N−1 states were computed on that fixed geometry).  The f+ descriptor indicates the β-carbon of the enal (atom 4, index 3, symbol C) is the most electrophilic site with f+ = 0.64232102; the carbonyl oxygen (atom 1, index 0, symbol O) is the most nucleophilic with f– = 0.39388616.  The full per-atom descriptors are included in the JSON result, together with the dual descriptor.

Method provenance
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f+, f–, f0) and dual descriptor
• Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted)  — chemkit tier `standard`
• Geometry provenance: single-point on supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz (no DFT re-optimization)
• Solvent: gas phase (no continuum model)
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states computed for f±
• Software: PySCF via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend version bundled with chemkit 1.0.0)
• Convergence: SCF for all three charge states converged; integrity check status OK, trustworthy = true.

Warnings (verbatim from engine):

2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
